text.title: growth-loops Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-loops as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring the quality of loop-acquired users by measuring only volume.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Viral loops that bring low-quality users (low activation, low retention) can grow the user count while damaging unit economics and product reputation. Measure activation rate of loop-acquired users separately from paid-acquired users.

failure-mode.description: Forcing unnatural or incentivized sharing that feels spammy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forced sharing (requiring users to tweet to unlock a feature) damages brand trust and produces low-quality referrals who churn immediately. Find genuine sharing moments where the user has a natural motivation to tell someone else.

failure-mode.description: Optimizing cycle time without measuring step conversions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Faster cycles of a loop with low efficiency produce no compounding. Step conversion rates determine the ceiling; cycle time only affects how fast you reach that ceiling.
